title: penstemon roseus
intro: Penstemon roseus, also known as the western prairie penstemon, is a perennial wildflower native to the Great Plains region of North America. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues, such as coughs and bronchitis, due to its expectorant properties, which help to loosen and clear mucus from the lungs. The health benefits of Penstemon roseus include its ability to reduce inflammation and improve respiratory function, making it a valuable herb in traditional medicine. Herbal preparations of the plant include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to make teas, salves, and other topical applications.
